Q1.title: Multiplication Table
Q1.description:
Read a number N using 🎤. Print the multiplication table of N from 1 to 5, one result per line.

Example:
Input:  3
Output: 3
        6
        9
        12
        15

Q2.title: Even or Odd
Q2.description:
Read a number N using 🎤. Print Even if it is even, or Odd if it is odd.

Example:
Input:  4
Output: Even

Input:  7
Output: Odd

Q3.title: Sum of Numbers
Q3.description:
Read N using 🎤, then read N numbers. Print the sum of all numbers.

Example:
Input:  4
        1
        2
        3
        4
Output: 10

Q4.title: Count Even Numbers
Q4.description:
Read N using 🎤, then read N numbers. Print the count of even numbers.

Example:
Input:  6
        1
        2
        3
        4
        5
        6
Output: 3

Q5.title: Factorial
Q5.description:
Read a number N using 🎤, print its factorial.
Remember: 0! = 1

Example:
Input:  5
Output: 120

Q6.title: Reverse String
Q6.description:
Read a string using 🎤, print it reversed.

Tip: Convert string to list, reverse using ↩️, then join with 🧩

Example:
Input:  HELLO
Output: OLLEH

Q7.title: Find Maximum
Q7.description:
Read N using 🎤, then read N numbers. Print the maximum value.

Tip: Use 🔺 to find max of a list

Example:
Input:  5
        3
        8
        2
        7
        5
Output: 8

Q8.title: Second Largest
Q8.description:
Read N using 🎤, then read N numbers. Print the second largest number. All numbers are distinct.

Tip: Sort the list using 🔃 then access by index

Example:
Input:  5
        3
        8
        2
        7
        5
Output: 7

Q9.title: Squares of Sorted Array
Q9.description:
Read N using 🎤, then read N numbers (may have negatives). Print the square of each number in sorted order, one per line.

Tip: Square each number using 🌟 2, sort using 🗃️

Example:
Input:  4
        -2
        0
        1
        3
Output: 0
        1
        4
        9

Q10.title: Missing Number
Q10.description:
Read N using 🎤, then read N numbers. The numbers are from 1 to N+1 but exactly one is missing. Find and print the missing number.

Tip: Expected sum of 1 to N+1 is (N+1)*(N+2)/2. Subtract actual sum.

Example:
Input:  4
        1
        2
        4
        5
Output: 3

Q11.title: Count Digits
Q11.description:
Read a number N using 🎤. Print the number of digits in it.

Example:
Input:  12345
Output: 5

Q12.title: Count Vowels
Q12.description:
Read a string using 🎤. Print the number of vowels (a, e, i, o, u — both upper and lower case).

Example:
Input:  Hello World
Output: 3

Q13.title: Sum of Digits
Q13.description:
Read a number N using 🎤. Print the sum of its digits.

Example:
Input:  1234
Output: 10

Q14.title: Palindrome Check
Q14.description:
Read a string using 🎤. Print Yes if it is a palindrome, otherwise print No. (Case sensitive)

Example:
Input:  racecar
Output: Yes

Input:  hello
Output: No

Q15.title: Fibonacci Series
Q15.description:
Read N using 🎤. Print the first N Fibonacci numbers, one per line, starting from 0.

Example:
Input:  6
Output: 0
        1
        1
        2
        3
        5

Q16.title: Double Exists
Q16.description:
Read N using 🎤, then read N numbers. Check if any number's double also exists in the array. Print Yes or No.

Example:
Input:  5
        2
        4
        5
        8
        10
Output: Yes
(4 is double of 2)

Q17.title: Prime Check
Q17.description:
Read a number N using 🎤. Print Yes if it is prime, otherwise print No.

Example:
Input:  7
Output: Yes

Input:  9
Output: No

Q18.title: List Average
Q18.description:
Read N using 🎤, then read N numbers. Print the average rounded to 1 decimal place.

Tip: Use 🔵 to round

Example:
Input:  4
        10
        20
        30
        40
Output: 25.0

Q19.title: Word Count
Q19.description:
Read a string using 🎤. Count how many words it contains (words are separated by spaces).

Example:
Input:  Hello World how are you
Output: 5

test_cases.input: a b c d e f

Q20.title: Sort and Print
Q20.description:
Read N using 🎤, then read N numbers. Print them in ascending order, one per line.

Tip: Use 🔃 to sort the list

Example:
Input:  5
        3
        1
        4
        1
        5
Output: 1
        1
        3
        4
        5
